an5.121:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Vesālī in the Gabled Hall at the Great Forest.
an5.121:1.2: Then, on emerging from his seclusion in the late afternoon, he went to the sick ward,
an5.121:1.3: where he saw a monk who was weak & sickly.
an5.121:1.4: Seeing him, he sat down on a prepared seat.
an5.121:1.5: Having sat down there, he addressed the monks:
an5.121:2.1: “When these five things don’t leave a monk who is weak & sickly, it can be expected of him that,
an5.121:2.2: before long—with the ending of the effluents—he will enter & remain in the effluent-free awareness-release & discernment-release, having directly known & realized them for himself right in the here & now.
an5.121:3.2: “There is the case where a monk [1] remains focused on unattractiveness with regard to the body, [2] is percipient of loathsomeness in food, [3] is percipient of distaste with regard to every world, [4] remains focused on inconstancy with regard to all fabrications. [5] The perception of death is well established within him.
an5.121:3.3: “When these five things don’t leave a monk who is weak & sickly, it can be expected of him that,
an5.121:3.4: before long—with the ending of the effluents—he will enter & remain in the effluent-free awareness-release & discernment-release, having directly known & realized them for himself right in the here & now.”
